text: The Pythagorean theorem describes the relationship between the sides of a right-angled triangle.
In this triangle, 'a' and 'b' are the legs, and 'c' is the hypotenuse.
The theorem states that the square of the hypotenuse is equal to the sum of the squares of the other two sides.
Mathematically, this is expressed as: a squared plus b squared equals c squared.
